medications.indication_rationale: Apply to lesions and ~2 cm beyond the border; continue ~1-2 weeks past clearance.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply twice daily to and beyond the border (as per source note)
10 mg orally daily (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Drives tinea incognito — must be stopped.
For extensive/refractory or markedly steroid-modified disease not clearing with topical therapy.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stop steroid creams, apply antifungal beyond the ring's edge, wash hands after touching the kitten/cream, don't share towels/clothing, and have the kitten vet-checked.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Halts spread, prevents transmission and reinfection.

patient_education_points.point: This is ringworm — a fungal infection (likely from your new kitten). The cortisone cream fed it and made it spread, so stop that. The antifungal cream kills it — apply it a couple of centimetres past the red ring.
Keep applying the antifungal for about a week after the skin looks clear, wash hands and don't share towels, and get the kitten checked by a vet.
If a ring becomes hot, swollen, oozes thick pus, or you develop a fever — seek medical care.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Resolution; consider oral antifungal if not clearing.

must_include_items: Diagnose tinea corporis (annular scaly advancing border) and STOP the topical steroid (tinea incognito)
Topical antifungal to and beyond the border, continued past clearance
Hygiene/no-sharing and vet check of the animal source
Secondary-infection safety-net (± oral antifungal if extensive/refractory)
acceptable_alternatives: Short oral antifungal for extensive/refractory disease
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires antifungal treatment (past the border) + stopping the steroid + source/hygiene advice, with a secondary-infection safety-net.
